description_long: This is a hand tool with a long, slender metallic shaft and a robust, faceted wooden handle. The handle is dark brown with visible wood grain and is shaped for a comfortable grip, tapering slightly toward the shaft. The shaft is metallic, straight, and appears to be made of steel, connecting securely to the handle with a metallic collar. The tip of the tool is narrow and appears to be a flathead, suitable for driving or removing slotted screws. The overall construction suggests durability and ergonomic use, with no visible branding or markings. The tool appears clean and well-maintained, with no visible signs of wear or damage.
description: A screwdriver with a faceted wooden handle and a long, straight metallic shaft ending in a flathead tip. The tool is clean, sturdy, and ergonomically shaped for hand use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the screwdriver, highlighting the faceted wooden handle and straight metallic shaft.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, displaying the faceted, rounded end and the transition to the shaft.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full length, with the flathead tip and handle profile visible.
description_view_3: View from the tip end, showing the flathead blade and the shaft extending into the handle.
attribute_handle_type: faceted, straight, wooden handle with visible grain and a slightly tapered ergonomic shape
